variables.content:
The longitudinal optical (LO) phonon mode with energy $\hbar\omega=18\ \text{meV}$ is defined as the lattice vibration whose eigenvector displaces Pb and Br atoms along specific directions in the orthorhombic cell, and prior experimental and theoretical evidence (including angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy and electron–phonon coupling calculations) identifies this 18 meV LO mode as the phonon most strongly coupled to charge carriers in CsPbBr₃; accordingly, displacing the lattice along the 18 meV LO eigenvector constitutes the physically motivated candidate to model a photoinduced polaronic structural distortion.
      Puppin et al., Phys. Rev. Lett. 2020; ARPES evidence cited in the paper

Using reported literature values of absolute energy-level positions for pure bulk $CsPbBr_3$ and $CsPbCl_3$ materials to construct an expected built-in potential for an ideal $CsPbBr_3/CsPbCl_3$ heterojunction by simple Fermi-level alignment yields an approximate predicted built-in potential of about $0.63\ \mathrm{eV}$ for idealized end-member junctions; however, the applicability of such bulk-derived absolute energy references to surface-sensitive, nanowire-exposed SPEM measurements is limited by surface dipoles, local stoichiometry ($x$ in $CsPb(Br_{1-x}Cl_x)_3$), defect states, and measurement calibration, so the literature-based value should be treated only as a rough reference for surface-sensitive experiments.
      [64]

Cesium lead bromide (CsPbBr_{3}) undergoes crystallographic phase changes with temperature and adopts an orthorhombic ground-state structure at room temperature, yet ARPES band dispersions measured at room temperature can in practice resemble higher-symmetry cubic-phase dispersions for some momentum-space cuts; therefore using an orthorhombic-structure G_{0}W_{0} calculation that yields m_{h}^{*}=0.226\,m_{0} for direct quantitative comparison with room-temperature ARPES data assumes that the orthorhombic theoretical band structure is an appropriate representation of the electronic states probed in the ARPES experiment, and further assumes that any difference in structural symmetry between the theoretical model and the actual electronic-state symmetry sampled by ARPES does not change the effective mass by an amount comparable to the experimental–theoretical difference being evaluated.

Under the scenario defined by holding every COVID‑19 epidemic influence coefficient equal to its calibrated 2020 numeric value for the years 2020–2022, the model projects percentage impacts attributable to COVID‑19 on Shanghai's main variables equal to: GDP change of −3.8% (2020), −7.4% (2021), and −10.9% (2022); Port throughput impact of −0.03% (2020), −0.08% (2021), and −0.1% (2022); Passenger transportation declines of −49.1% (2020), −49.2% (2021), and −49.1% (2022); and Cargo transportation declines of −10.6% (2020), −20.1% (2021), and −28.6% (2022).

Using ab initio molecular dynamics (AIMD) sampling of finite-temperature atomic configurations for bulk CsPbBr₃ at temperature T = 300 K in the canonical (NVT) ensemble with a Nosé–Hoover thermostat on a 3×3×3 pseudocubic supercell (135 atoms) and a 1 fs time step, and computing single-point electronic structures on a representative equilibrated AIMD snapshot with the Heyd–Scuseria–Ernzerhof hybrid functional including spin–orbit coupling (HSE–SOC, where HSE denotes the screened hybrid functional of Heyd, Scuseria, and Ernzerhof and SOC denotes spin–orbit coupling) using a hybrid mixing parameter α = 0.54 chosen so that the calculated band gap E_g = 2.37 eV matches the experimental room-temperature band gap, the direction-averaged hole effective mass computed for that room-temperature AIMD configuration is m_h = 0.265 m_0 (where m_0 is the free-electron mass). This computed m_h = 0.265 m_0 agrees with the angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES) measurement of m_h = 0.26 ± 0.02 m_0 reported for CsPbBr₃, and this agreement is obtained without introducing any extra hole into the supercell (i.e., without polaron formation); therefore the ARPES-measured hole effective mass can be explained by thermal-lattice fluctuations alone under these simulated room-temperature, bulk-like conditions.

Under a scenario in which every COVID‑19 epidemic influence coefficient is uniformly decreased by 10% relative to its 2020 calibrated value for 2020–2022, the model projects percentage impacts on Shanghai's variables of: GDP change of −3.8% (2020), −1.8% (2021), and +0.04% (2022); Port throughput impact of +0.03% (2020), −0.4% (2021), and −1.3% (2022); Passenger transportation declines of −49.1% (2020), −32.7% (2021), and −7.8% (2022); and Cargo transportation declines of −10.6% (2020), −6.8% (2021), and −2.0% (2022).

For cubic CsPbI2Br measured at 2 K, the band-gap energy determined from the 1s excitonic absorption is E_g = 1906 meV, the exciton binding energy determined from the hydrogenic exciton fits is R* = 22 ± 3 meV, the exciton reduced mass determined from the high-field inter–Landau-level fits is μ = 0.124 ± 0.02 m_0, and the effective dielectric constant inferred from R* via R* = R_0 (μ/m_0) / ε_eff^2 is ε_eff = 8.6.

The reported impact-parameter measurements and $1\sigma$ uncertainties are $b_{2019}=0.91497_{-0.00089}^{+0.00093}$ ($\sigma_{2019}\approx0.00091$), $b_{2020}=0.8953_{-0.0048}^{+0.0050}$ ($\sigma_{2020}\approx0.0049$), and $b_{2022}=0.8797\pm0.0011$ ($\sigma_{2022}=0.0011$); the proposition is that these quoted uncertainties and the inverse-variance weights $w_i=1/\sigma_{i}^2$ derived from them adequately represent both statistical measurement errors and instrument-to-instrument systematic offsets (including the 2020 CARMENES–EXPRES disagreement), so that the weighted least-squares slope estimate $db/dt$ and its formal uncertainty are valid for hypothesis testing and significance statements about the detected trend.

The species identity of a male beetle specimen can be determined as Ptilodactyla exotica Chapin, 1927 by visually comparing the specimen's aedeagus (the intromittent male genital sclerite, here examined in dorsal view) to published aedeagal illustrations attributed to Ptilodactyla exotica in Chapin (1927), Binaghi (1972), Süss & Puppin (1976), Aberlenc & Allemand (1997), Mann (2006), and Viñolas et al. (2020); this comparison is performed by checking congruence of the aedeagal median lobe shape, lateral lobes, apical configuration, and any associated ventral sclerites between the voucher specimen and those figures.
      Chapin 1927; Binaghi 1972; Süss & Puppin 1976; Aberlenc & Allemand 1997; Mann 2006; Viñolas et al. 2020

The GGA + mBJ band‑gap estimates obtained with the full‑potential linearized augmented plane‑wave (FP‑LAPW) implementation reproduce the experimental trend qualitatively where comparison data exist; for example, the computed E_g = 1.85 eV for CsPbI₂Br agrees closely with a cited experimental value of 1.92 eV, indicating qualitative agreement between the FP‑LAPW GGA + mBJ band gaps and available experimental measurements for these compositions.
